macro_segments.title: The basket of harlequin ducks and the examination of the sinew cluster

micro_segments.title: Identification of the inkurei birds as harlequin ducks with white splotches
Rava's examination of the tsomet hagidim (cluster of sinews) in the bird's leg
Explanation of the tsomet hagidim in mammals versus birds and its role as a potential treifah

macro_segments.title: Dislocated hind leg in animals and birds: the application of mammalian tereifot to birds

micro_segments.title: Teaching of Rav Yehuda in the name of Rav: dislocated forearm kosher, dislocated hind leg treifah in animals
Application to birds: dislocated thigh bone and the concern about lung puncture
Dislocated wing of a bird and fear of lung puncture; testing versus declaring treifah

macro_segments.title: The debate about whether birds have lungs

micro_segments.title: Chizkiya's claim that birds do not have lungs; Rebbe Yochanan's response with empirical evidence
Interpretation of Chizkiya's statement: lungs are not a concern in specific scenarios (falling, burning)
Dismissal of Chizkiya as ignorant of birds despite his opinions

macro_segments.title: Conflicting traditions about whether a dislocated thigh in a bird is kosher or treifah

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna's teaching in the name of Rav: dislocated thigh of a bird is kosher
Raba bar Rav Huna challenges his father with a different tradition in the name of Rav: dislocated thigh is treifah
Rav Huna's response using the metaphor of rivers and tributaries; reconciling different practices
Rabbi Abba's inquiry into the צומת הגידים and challenge to Rav Yirmiya bar Abba
Rabbi Yirmiya bar Abba proposes a distinction between dislocated (kosher) and severed (treifah)
Rabbi Abba confirms Rav's explicit distinction: dislocated is kosher, severed is treifah

macro_segments.title: The story of Rabbi Abba reporting to Rabbi Zeira in Eretz Yisrael about the dislocated thigh debate

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Abba recounts the entire dislocated thigh discussion to Rabbi Zeira
Rabbi Zeira presents alternative traditions from Eretz Yisrael: Rav Chiya bar Ashi and Rabbi Yaakov bar Idi in the name of Rabbi Yochanan hold dislocated thigh is treifah
Story of Rabbi Chanina's chicken with dislocated thigh approved by Rebbi; Rabbi Chanina preserves it as teaching tool

macro_segments.title: The ruling that dislocated thigh is treifah despite evidence to the contrary

micro_segments.title: The Gemara rejects all the previous discussion and establishes the widespread practice: dislocated thigh is treifah
Story of Rabbi Yossi ben Nehorai and Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi discussing hole sizes in the trachea
Evidence of a sheep surviving with a reed splint covering a tracheal hole; response that it must have died within twelve months
Introduction of the twelve-month timeframe: a treifah dies within twelve months, not immediately

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon ben Chalafta's empirical tests to disprove halachic positions

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon ben Chalafta's test of Rabbi Yehuda's position that a bird missing down is treifah
The chicken survives and regrows better feathers; Shimon concludes Rabbi Yehuda is wrong
Discussion of whether a treifah can improve (טריפה משבחת) and the implications for Rabbi Yehuda's ruling

macro_segments.title: Shimon ben Chalafta's experiment with ants testing the absence of an ant king

micro_segments.title: Reference to Proverbs about the ant working without a king; Shimon's design to test this claim
Shimon spreads his cloak over an anthill, tags a scout ant, and observes its communication to other ants
Ants kill the scout ant upon discovering the deception; Shimon concludes ants have no king
Rav Acha's challenges to Shimon's proof: the king could have been present, had preauthorization, or the period lacked a king

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's response: trust authority rather than empirical evidence

micro_segments.title: The Gemara advises to trust King Solomon and the sages rather than conduct experiments
Tosafot's interpretation: Shimon was trying to confirm the sages, not disprove them, but empirical testing is still unnecessary
Parallel story of a student who mocked the words of sages by demanding empirical proof; Rabbi Yochanan rebuked him
Discussion of the tension between authority and observation in halacha; preview of the twelve-month timeframe discussion

Application of mammalian tereifot to birds
Rebbe Yehuda's position: missing down is treifah

Rabbi Abba, Rabbi Zeira, Rav Yirmiya bar Abba dialogue
Rabbi Chanina's chicken with dislocated thigh
Trachea hole and reed splint evidence
Rabbi Shimon ben Chalafta as askan bidvarim (tester of things)
Ant experiment: king vs. no king in Proverbs
